segments.text: I hate my life! Let me speak freely about my complaints—I can't keep my bitterness to myself.
I will tell God, {
Don't just condemn me—tell me what you have against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you enjoy accusing me? Why do you reject me, someone you made with your own hands, and yet smile on the scheming of the wicked?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you have human eyes? Do you see like human beings do?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is your life as short as mortal beings? Are your years as brief as those of humanity,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that you have to examine my wrongs and investigate my sins?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though you know I'm not guilty, no one can save me from you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You made me and shaped me with your own hands, and yet you destroy me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Remember that you shaped me like a piece of clay—are you now going to turn me back into dust?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You poured me out like milk, you curdled me like cheese.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You clothed me with skin and flesh; you wove my body together with bones and muscles.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You granted me life and showed me your kindness; you have taken great care of me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But you kept these things hidden in your heart. I know your purpose was
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: to watch me, and if I sinned, then you would not forgive my wrongs.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I'm guilty I'm in trouble, if I'm innocent I can't hold my head high because I'm totally disgraced as I look at my sufferings.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I do hold my head high you hunt me down like a lion, showing how powerful you are in hurting me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You repeat your arguments against me, you pour out more and more of your anger against me, you send fresh armies against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: So why did you allow me to be born? I wish that I had died, and nobody had ever seen me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It would have been better if I had never existed, taken straight from the womb to the grave.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I only have a few days left, so why don't you leave me alone so I can have a little peace
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: before I go to where I shall never return from, the land of darkness and the shadow of death—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: the land of total darkness where death's shadow lies, a place of chaos where light itself is darkness.
segments.speaker: Job
